mentions.note: Strait of Hormuz closure driving record petrochemical spreads; ethylene/polyethylene margins lifted substantially. Q2 guidance $12B revenue, $2B EBITDA; trajectory implies $6B+ annual EBITDA. Up 65% YTD; succession planning intact.
Commodity chemical maker benefiting from war-driven supply destruction; price hikes should persist as capacity remains offline.
Up 78% Q1 on petrochemical shortage; benefits from rate-cut expectations and Iranian supply disruption; at risk if strait reopens.
Caller asked for diversification; had nice bounce on cyclical demand but trimmer here not buyer; still needs Chinese buyers to return from weak position.
